pages.full_text:
We left the campsite + went up a winding valley with spectacular waterfalls + rickety bridges, paths + ladders for fishmen.
     The valley opened out for a fascinating old church - Borgund stave church. It smells woody + of pitch. Dark + cosy. There are photos of funerals + christenings. In 1820 people were burnt to death in a stave church, so the gov't decreed that all church doors should open outwards + the church was modified. It was also 1800s laid down that every church should have seats for 1/3 local population. So the parishioners built another church nearby, + the old church remained.
     We came back down the valley + went to shops at __Laerdal__ + the salmon museum. In the 1980's salmon developed a parasite. The only way to kill the parasite is to kill off everything in the river, using a plant mixture. The rivers take about 4 years to get life back.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number clearly visible at top-left corner — top of image is slightly cut off, making it uncertain. Text continues from a previous page (no heading visible at start). 'fishmen' likely means fishermen (abbreviated or mis-written). 'gov't' written as an abbreviation. '1800s' appears in margin/inline — possibly '1800's' or referencing the 19th century generally. 'Laerdal' is underlined in the original; in modern spelling Lærdal (Laerdal). The salmon museum mentioned is the Norsk Villakssenter (Norwegian Wild Salmon Centre) in Lærdal. Year cannot be firmly established from this page alone; Norway trip context and volume position suggest late 1990s or early 2000s.

pages.full_text:
__Borgund stave church__ c 11.50.
lovely smell of pitch. Dark + woody inside.
Old photos of christenings, weddings + funerals.
     Only 2? out of 1000 stave churches remain.
photo_captions: Borgund stave church c 11.50. lovely smell of pitch. Dark + woody inside. Old photos of christenings, weddings + funerals. Only 2? out of 1000 stave churches remain.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a large colour photograph of Borgund stave church, taken from outside showing the multi-tiered wooden structure with mountains behind. The handwritten caption below the photo is underlined at 'Borgund stave church'. 'c 11.50' likely refers to the entry cost (£11.50 or similar in local currency). 'Only 2?' — the '?' may be part of the original (uncertain number) or could read '27' or another number; the text reads as if uncertain how many stave churches remain. No handwritten page number clearly visible in top-right corner (area above photo is cut off in scan).
